reasoning: Aluminium sodium dioxide (NaAlO2, sodium aluminate) is an inorganic compound containing both a metal (sodium, aluminium) and a non-metal (oxygen) component, forming an aluminate salt. It fits best under mixed metal/non-metal compounds, specifically as a miscellaneous mixed metal/non-metal since no more specific label for aluminates involving both alkali metals and post-transition metals exists in the taxonomy.
